Requirement: The platform shall support multiple independently configured MCP server connections.
Acceptance criterion: FastMCP, n8n, and one standalone server are connected concurrently with independent health and authentication profiles.

Requirement: All discovered tools shall pass a deny-by-default registration and allowlist process before model or graph exposure.
Acceptance criterion: An unregistered discovered tool is unavailable and its attempted exposure is logged.

Requirement: Tool identifiers shall be globally unique and namespaced by server domain.
Acceptance criterion: Two servers exposing the same local tool name are registered without collision and remain independently addressable.

Requirement: n8n workflows exposed as Copilot tools shall have versioned typed contracts and governance metadata.
Acceptance criterion: A workflow lacking owner schema risk approval idempotency or lifecycle metadata fails registration.

Requirement: Asynchronous n8n execution shall use durable correlation authenticated callbacks and idempotent completion handling.
Acceptance criterion: A duplicate or replayed callback does not resume or execute the LangGraph workflow more than once.

Requirement: n8n event triggers shall create governed Copilot work items and shall not bypass policy or approval.
Acceptance criterion: An incoming exception event is evaluated and queued but cannot execute a restricted SAP write without policy and approval.

Requirement: Credentials shall remain isolated in the integration runtime that owns them and shall not be copied to model prompts.
Acceptance criterion: Prompt trace and LangGraph state inspection contain no raw n8n FastMCP standalone-server or SAP credentials.

Requirement: Traces shall link LangGraph MCP n8n and SAP or external-system execution identifiers.
Acceptance criterion: A completed delegated workflow is reconstructed end to end from one HANA-X correlation ID.

Requirement: The platform shall monitor server health capability version and tool inventory for every configured MCP connection.
Acceptance criterion: A failed or incompatible server is removed from eligible routing and an operational alert is recorded.
